{"meta": null, "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "{"content":"1100: \n1101:   // Place pseudo probe instrumentation as the first pass of the pipeline to\n1102:   // minimize the impact of optimization changes.\n1103:   if (PGOOpt \u0026\u0026 PGOOpt-\u003ePseudoProbeForProfiling \u0026\u0026\n1104:       Phase != ThinOrFullLTOPhase::ThinLTOPostLink)\n1105:     MPM.addPass(SampleProfileProbePass(TM));\n1106: \n1107:   bool HasSampleProfile = PGOOpt \u0026\u0026 (PGOOpt-\u003eAction == PGOOptions::SampleUse);\n1108: \n1109:   // In ThinLTO mode, when flattened profile is used, all the available\n1110:   // profile information will be annotated in PreLink phase so there is\n1111:   // no need to load the profile again in PostLink.\n1112:   bool LoadSampleProfile =\n1113:       HasSampleProfile \u0026\u0026\n1114:       !(FlattenedProfileUsed \u0026\u0026 Phase == ThinOrFullLTOPhase::ThinLTOPostLink);\n1115: \n1116:   // During the ThinLTO backend phase we perform early indirect call promotion\n1117:   // here, before globalopt. Otherwise imported available_externally functions\n1118:   // look unreferenced and are removed. If we are going to load the sample\n1119:   // profile then defer until later.\n1120:   // TODO: See if we can move later and consolidate with the location where\n1121:   // we perform ICP when we are loading a sample profile.\n1122:   // TODO: We pass HasSampleProfile (whether there was a sample profile file\n1123:   // passed to the compile) to the SamplePGO flag of ICP. This is used to\n1124:   // determine whether the new direct calls are annotated with prof metadata.\n1125:   // Ideally this should be determined from whether the IR is annotated with\n1126:   // sample profile, and not whether the a sample profile was provided on the\n1127:   // command line. E.g. for flattened profiles where we will not be reloading\n1128:   // the sample profile in the ThinLTO backend, we ideally shouldn't have to\n1129:   // provide the sample profile file.\n1130:   if (Phase == ThinOrFullLTOPhase::ThinLTOPostLink \u0026\u0026 !LoadSampleProfile)\n1131:     MPM.addPass(PGOIndirectCallPromotion(true /* InLTO */, HasSampleProfile));\n1132: \n1133:   // Create an early function pass manager to cleanup the output of the\n1134:   // frontend. Not necessary with LTO post link pipelines since the pre link\n1135:   // pipeline already cleaned up the frontend output.\n1136:   if (Phase != ThinOrFullLTOPhase::ThinLTOPostLink) {\n1137:     // Do This is\n1602:   // currently only performed for loops marked with the metadata\n1603:   // llvm.loop.distribute=true or when -enable-loop-distribute is specified.\n1604:   OptimizePM.addPass(LoopDistributePass());\n1605: \n1606:   // Populates the VFABI attribute with the scalar-to-vector mappings\n1607:   // from the TargetLibraryInfo.\n1608:   OptimizePM.addPass(InjectTLIMappings());\n1609: \n1610:   addVectorPasses(Level, OptimizePM, LTOPhase);\n1611: \n1612:   invokeVectorizerEndEPCallbacks(OptimizePM, Level);\n1613: \n1614:   // LoopSink pass sinks instructions hoisted by LICM, which serves as a\n1615:   // canonicalization pass that enables other optimizations. It should run after other sink/hoist\n1624:   // passes to avoid re-sinking, but before SimplifyCFG because it can allow\n1625:   // flattening of blocks.\n1626:   OptimizePM.addPass(DivRemPairsPass());\n1627: \n1628:   // Try to annotate calls that were created during optimization.\n1629:   OptimizePM.addPass(\n1630:       TailCallElimPass(/*UpdateFunctionEntryCount=*/isInstrumentedPGOUse()));\n1631: \n1632:   // LoopSink (and other loop passes since the last simplifyCFG) might have\n1633:   // resulted in single-entry-single-exit or empty blocks. 1501:   // attributes where applicable.

1503:   MPM.addPass(ReversePostOrderFunctionAttrsPass());
1504: 
1505:   // Do a post inline PGO instrumentation and use pass. This is a context
1506:   // sensitive PGO pass. We don't want to do this in LTOPreLink phrase as
1507:   // cross-module inline has not been done yet. The context sensitive
1508:   // instrumentation is after all the inlines are done.
1509:   if (!LTOPreLink && PGOOpt) {
1510:     if (PGOOpt->CSAction == PGOOptions::CSIRInstr)
1511:       addPGOInstrPasses(MPM, Level, /*RunProfileGen=*/true,
1512:                         /*IsCS=*/true, PGOOpt->AtomicCounterUpdate,
1513:                         PGOOpt->CSProfileGenFile, PGOOpt->ProfileRemappingFile);
1514:     else if (PGOOpt->CSAction == PGOOptions::CSIRUse)
1515:       addPGOInstrPasses(MPM, Level, /*RunProfileGen=*/false,
1516:                         /*IsCS=*/true, PGOOpt->AtomicCounterUpdate,
1517:                         PGOOpt->ProfileFile, PGOOpt->ProfileRemappingFile);
1518:   }
1519: 
1520:   // Re-compute GlobalsAA here prior to function passes. This is particularly
1521:   // useful as the above will have inlined, DCE'ed, and function-attr
1522:   // propagated everything. We should at this point have a reasonably minimal
1523:   // and richly annotated call graph. By computing aliasing and mod/ref
1524:   // information for all local globals here, the late loop passes and notably
1525:   // the vectorizer will be able to use them to help recognize vectorizable
1526:   // memory operations.
1527:   if (EnableGlobalAnalyses)
1528:     MPM.addPass(RecomputeGlobalsAAPass());
1529: 
1530:   invokeOptimizerEarlyEPCallbacks(MPM, Level, LTOPhase);
1531: 
1532:   FunctionPassManager OptimizePM;
1533: 
1534:   // Only drop unnecessary assumes post-inline and post-link, as otherwise
1535:   // additional uses of the affected value may be introduced through inlining
1536:   // and CSE.
1537:   if (!isLTOPreLink(LTOPhase))
1538:     OptimizePM.addPass(DropUnnecessaryAssumesPass());
1539: 
1540:   // Scheduling LoopVersioningLICM when inlining is over, because after that
1541:   // we may see more accurate aliasing. Reason to run this late is that too
1542:   // early versioning may prevent further inlining due to increase of code
1543:   // size. Other optimizations which runs later might get benefit of no-alias
1544:   // assumption in clone loop.
1545:   if (UseLoopVersioningLICM) {
1546:     OptimizePM.addPass(
1547:         createFunctionToLoopPassAdaptor(LoopVersioningLICMPass()));
1548:     // LoopVersioningLICM pass might increase new LICM opportunities.
1549:     OptimizePM.addPass(createFunctionToLoopPassAdaptor(
1550:         LICMPass(PTO.LicmMssaOptCap, PTO.LicmMssaNoAccForPromotionCap,
1551:                  /*AllowSpeculation=*/true),
1552:         /*USeMemorySSA=*/true));
1553:   }
1554: 
1555:   OptimizePM.addPass(Float2IntPass());
1556:   OptimizePM.addPass(LowerConstantIntrinsicsPass());
1557: 
1558:   if (EnableMatrix) {
1559:     OptimizePM.addPass(LowerMatrixIntrinsicsPass());
1560:     OptimizePM.addPass(EarlyCSEPass());
1561:   }
1562: 
1563:   // CHR pass should only be applied with the profile information.
1564:   // The check is to check the profile summary information in CHR.
1565:   if (EnableCHR && Level == OptimizationLevel::O3)
1566:     OptimizePM.addPass(ControlHeightReductionPass());
1567: 
1568:   // FIXME: We need to run some loop optimizations to re-rotate loops after
1569:   // simplifycfg and others undo their rotation.
1570: 
1571:   // Optimize the loop execution. These passes operate on entire loop nests
1572:   // rather than on each loop in an inside-out manner, and so they are actually
1573:   // function passes.
1574: 
1575:   invokeVectorizerStartEPCallbacks(OptimizePM, Level);
1576: 
1577:   LoopPassManager LPM;
1578:   // First rotate loops that may have been un-rotated by prior passes.
1579:   // Disable header duplication at -Oz.
1580:   LPM.addPass(LoopRotatePass(EnableLoopHeaderDuplication ||
1581:                                  Level != OptimizationLevel::Oz,
1582:                              LTOPreLink));

1586:   //        in the simplification pipeline.
1587:   LPM.addPass(LoopDeletionPass());

1589:   if (PTO.LoopInterchange)

1591: 
1592:   OptimizePM.addPass(
1593:       createFunctionToLoopPassAdaptor(std::move(LPM), /*UseMemorySSA=*/false));

1595:   // FIXME: This may not be the right place in the pipeline.

1600:   // Distribute loops to allow partial vectorization.  I.e. isolate dependences
1601:   // into separate loop that would otherwise inhibit vectorization.  This is

1603:   // llvm.loop.distribute=true or when -enable-loop-distribute is specified.
1604:   OptimizePM.addPass(LoopDistributePass());
1605: 
1606:   // Populates the VFABI attribute with the scalar-to-vector mappings
1607:   // from the TargetLibraryInfo.
1608:   OptimizePM.addPass(InjectTLIMappings());
1609: 
1610:   addVectorPasses(Level, OptimizePM, LTOPhase);
1611: 
1612:   invokeVectorizerEndEPCallbacks(OptimizePM, Level);
1613: 
1614:   // LoopSink pass sinks instructions hoisted by LICM, which serves as a
1615:   // canonicalization pass that enables other optimizations. As a result,
1616:   // LoopSink pass needs to be a very late IR pass to avoid undoing LICM
1617:   // result too early.
1618:   OptimizePM.addPass(LoopSinkPass());
1619: 
1620:   // And finally clean up LCSSA form before generating code.
1621:   OptimizePM.addPass(InstSimplifyPass());
1622: 
1623:   // This hoists/decomposes div/rem ops. It should run after other sink/hoist
1624:   // passes to avoid re-sinking, but before SimplifyCFG because it can allow
1625:   // flattening of blocks.
1626:   OptimizePM.addPass(DivRemPairsPass());
1627: 
1628:   // Try to annotate calls that were created during optimization.
1629:   OptimizePM.addPass(
1630:       TailCallElimPass(/*UpdateFunctionEntryCount=*/isInstrumentedPGOUse()));
1631: 
1632:   // LoopSink (and other loop passes since the last simplifyCFG) might have
1633:   // resulted in single-entry-single-exit or empty blocks. Clean up the CFG.
1634:   OptimizePM.addPass(
1635:       SimplifyCFGPass(SimplifyCFGOptions()
1636:                           .convertSwitchRangeToICmp(true)
1637:                           .convertSwitchToArithmetic(true)
1638:                           .speculateUnpredictables(true)
1639:                           .hoistLoadsStoresWithCondFaulting(true)));
1640: 
1641:   // Add the core optimizing pipeline.
1642:   MPM.addPass(createModuleToFunctionPassAdaptor(std::move(OptimizePM),
1643:                                                 PTO.EagerlyInvalidateAnalyses));
1644: 
1645:   // AllocToken transforms heap allocation calls; this needs to run late after
1646:   // other allocation call transformations (such as those in InstCombine).
1647:   if (!LTOPreLink)
1648:     MPM.addPass(AllocTokenPass());

1650:   invokeOptimizerLastEPCallbacks(MPM, Level, LTOPhase);
1651: 
1652:   // Split out cold code. Splitting is done late to avoid hiding context from
1653:   // other optimizations and inadvertently regressing performance. The tradeoff
1654:   // is that this has a higher code size cost than splitting early.
1655:   if (EnableHotColdSplit && !LTOPreLink)
1656:     MPM.addPass(HotColdSplittingPass());
1657: 
1658:   // Search the code for similar regions of code. If enough similar regions can
1659:   // be found where extracting the regions into their own function will decrease
1660:   // the size of the program, we extract the regions, a deduplicate the
1661:   // structurally similar regions.

1668:   // ordering here.
1669:   MPM.addPass(GlobalDCEPass());

1674:   if (PTO.MergeFunctions)

1685:   // the pass is already enabled in the LTO pipeline.
1686:   if (PTO.DevirtualizeSpeculatively && LTOPhase == ThinOrFullLTOPhase::None) {
1687:     // TODO: explore a better pipeline configuration that can improve
1688:     // compilation time overhead.

The \\c PassRegistry.def file specifies how to construct all\n112: /// of the built-in passes, and those may reference these members during\n113: /// construction.\n114: class PassBuilder {\n115:   TargetMachine *TM;\n116:   PipelineTuningOptions PTO;\n117:   std::optional\u003cPGOOptions\u003e PGOOpt;\n118:   PassInstrumentationCallbacks *PIC;\n119:   IntrusiveRefCntPtr\u003cvfs::FileSystem\u003e FS;\n120: \n121: public:\n122:   /// A struct to capture parsed pass pipeline names.\n123:   ///\n124:   /// A pipeline is defined as a series of names, each of which may in itself\n125:   /// recursively contain a nested pipeline. A name is either the name of a pass\n126:   /// (e.g. \"instcombine\") or the name of a pipeline type (e.g. \"cgscc\"). If the\n127:   /// name is the name of a pass, the InnerPipeline is empty, since passes\n128:   /// cannot contain inner pipelines. See parsePassPipeline() for a more\n129:   /// detailed description of the textual pipeline format.\n130:   struct PipelineElement {\n131:     StringRef Name;\n132:     std::vector\u003cPipelineElement\u003e InnerPipeline;\n133:   };\n134: \n135:   LLVM_ABI explicit PassBuilder(\n136:       TargetMachine *TM = nullptr,\n137:       PipelineTuningOptions PTO = PipelineTuningOptions(),\n138:       std::optional\u003cPGOOptions\u003e PGOOpt = std::nullopt,\n139:       PassInstrumentationCallbacks *PIC = nullptr,\n140:       IntrusiveRefCntPtr\u003cvfs::FileSystem\u003e FS = vfs::getRealFileSystem());\n141: \n142:   /// Cross register the analysis managers through their proxies.\n143:   ///\n144:   /// This is an interface that can be used to cross register each\n145:   /// AnalysisManager with all the others analysis managers.\n146:   LLVM_ABI void\n147:   crossRegisterProxies(LoopAnalysisManager \u0026LAM, FunctionAnalysisManager \u0026FAM,\n148:                        CGSCCAnalysisManager \u0026CGAM, ModuleAnalysisManager \u0026MAM,\n149:                        MachineFunctionAnalysisManager *MFAM = nullptr);\n150: \n151:   /// Registers all available module analysis passes.\n152:   ///\n153:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \\c\n154:   /// ModuleAnalysisManager with all registered module analyses. Callers can\n155:   /// still manually register any additional analyses. Callers can also\n156:   /// pre-register analyses and this will not override those.\n157:   LLVM_ABI void registerModuleAnalyses(ModuleAnalysisManager \u0026MAM);\n158: \n159:   /// Registers all available CGSCC analysis passes.\n160:   ///\n161:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \\c CGSCCAnalysisManager\n162:   /// with all registered CGSCC analyses. Callers can still manually register any\n163:   /// additional analyses. Callers can also pre-register analyses and this will\n164:   /// not override those.\n165:   LLVM_ABI void registerCGSCCAnalyses(CGSCCAnalysisManager \u0026CGAM);\n166: \n167:   /// Registers all available function analysis passes.\n168:   ///\n169:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \\c\n170:   /// FunctionAnalysisManager with all registered function analyses. Callers can\n171:   /// still manually register any additional analyses. Callers can also\n172:   /// pre-register analyses and this will not override those.\n173:   LLVM_ABI void registerFunctionAnalyses(FunctionAnalysisManager \u0026FAM);\n174: \n175:   /// Registers all available loop analysis passes.\n176:   ///\n177:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \\c LoopAnalysisManager\n178:   /// with all registered loop analyses. Callers can still manually register any\n179:   /// additional analyses.\n180:   LLVM_ABI void registerLoopAnalyses(LoopAnalysisManager \u0026LAM);\n181: \n182:   /// Registers all available machine function analysis passes.\n183:   ///\n184:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \\c\n185:   /// MachineFunctionAnalysisManager with all registered function analyses.\n186:   /// Callers can still manually register any additional analyses. Callers can\n187:   /// also pre-register analyses and this will not override those.\n188:   LLVM_ABI void\n189:   registerMachineFunctionAnalyses(MachineFunctionAnalysisManager \u0026MFAM);\n190: \n191:   /// Construct the core LLVM function canonicalization and simplification\n192:   /// pipeline.\n193:   ///\n194:   /// This is a long pipeline and uses most of the per-function optimization\n195:   /// passes in LLVM to canonicalize and simplify the IR. It is suitable to run\n196:   /// repeatedly over the IR and is not expected to destroy important\n197:   /// information about the semantics of the IR.\n198:   ///\n199:   /// Note that \\p Level cannot be `O0` here. The pipelines produced are\n200:   /// only intended for use when attempting to optimize code. If frontends\n201:   /// require some transformations for semantic reasons, they should explicitly\n202:   /// build them.\n203:   ///\n204:   /// \\p Phase indicates the current ThinLTO phase.\n205:   LLVM_ABI FunctionPassManager buildFunctionSimplificationPipeline(\n206:       OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase);\n207: \n208:   /// Construct the core LLVM module canonicalization and simplification\n209:   /// pipeline.\n210:   ///\n211:   /// This pipeline focuses on canonicalizing and simplifying the entire module\n212:   /// of IR. Much like the function simplification pipeline above, it is\n213:   /// suitable to run repeatedly over the IR and is not expected to destroy\n214:   /// important information. It does, however, perform inlining and other\n215:   /// heuristic based simplifications that are not strictly reversible.\n216:   ///\n217:   /// Note that \\p Level cannot be `O0` here. The pipelines produced are\n218:   /// only intended for use when attempting to optimize code. If frontends\n219:   /// require some transformations for semantic reasons, they should explicitly\n220:   /// build them.\n221:   ///\n222:   /// \\p Phase indicates the current ThinLTO phase.\n223:   LLVM_ABI ModulePassManager buildModuleSimplificationPipeline(\n224:       OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase);\n225: \n226:   /// Construct the module pipeline that performs inlining as well as\n227:   /// the inlining-driven cleanups.\n228:   LLVM_ABI ModuleInlinerWrapperPass\n229:   buildInlinerPipeline(OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase);\n230: \n231:   /// Construct the module pipeline that performs inlining with\n232:   /// module inliner pass.\n233:   LLVM_ABI ModulePassManager\n234:   buildModuleInlinerPipeline(OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase);\n235: \n236:   /// Construct the core LLVM module optimization pipeline.\n237:   ///\n238:   /// This pipeline focuses on optimizing the execution speed of the IR. It\n239:   /// uses cost modeling and thresholds to balance code growth against runtime\n240:   /// improvements. It includes vectorization and other information destroying\n241:   /// transformations. It also cannot generally be run repeatedly on a module\n242:   /// without potentially seriously regressing either runtime performance of\n243:   /// the code or serious code size growth.\n244:   ///\n245:   /// Note that \\p Level cannot be `O0` here. The pipelines produced are\n246:   /// only intended for use when attempting to optimize code. If frontends\n247:   /// require some transformations for semantic reasons, they should explicitly\n248:   /// build them.\n249:   LLVM_ABI ModulePassManager buildModuleOptimizationPipeline(\n250:       OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase LTOPhase);\n251: \n252:   /// Build a per-module default optimization pipeline.\n253:   ///\n254:   /// This provides a good default optimization pipeline for per-module\n255:   /// optimization and code generation without any link-time optimization. It\n256:   /// typically correspond to frontend \"-O[123]\" options for optimization\n257:   /// levels \\c O1, \\c O2 and \\c O3 resp.\n258:   LLVM_ABI ModulePassManager buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline(\n259:       OptimizationLevel Level,\n260:       ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase = ThinOrFullLTOPhase::None);\n261: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d@4152de6435984e9fc55431f4d74a7470d8ac05c0/-/blob/llvm/include/llvm/Passes/PassBuilder.h%3FL100-260"}", "annotations": null, "meta": null}], "structuredContent": {"content": "100:   // default pipelines.

108: /// This class provides access to building LLVM's passes.
109: ///
110: /// Its members provide the baseline state available to passes during their
111: /// construction. The \c PassRegistry.def file specifies how to construct all
112: /// of the built-in passes, and those may reference these members during
113: /// construction.
114: class PassBuilder {
115:   TargetMachine *TM;
116:   PipelineTuningOptions PTO;
117:   std::optional<PGOOptions> PGOOpt;
118:   PassInstrumentationCallbacks *PIC;
119:   IntrusiveRefCntPtr<vfs::FileSystem> FS;
120: 
121: public:
122:   /// A struct to capture parsed pass pipeline names.
123:   ///
124:   /// A pipeline is defined as a series of names, each of which may in itself
125:   /// recursively contain a nested pipeline. A name is either the name of a pass
126:   /// (e.g. "instcombine") or the name of a pipeline type (e.g. "cgscc"). If the
127:   /// name is the name of a pass, the InnerPipeline is empty, since passes
128:   /// cannot contain inner pipelines. See parsePassPipeline() for a more
129:   /// detailed description of the textual pipeline format.
130:   struct PipelineElement {
131:     StringRef Name;
132:     std::vector<PipelineElement> InnerPipeline;
133:   };
134: 
135:   LLVM_ABI explicit PassBuilder(
136:       TargetMachine *TM = nullptr,
137:       PipelineTuningOptions PTO = PipelineTuningOptions(),
138:       std::optional<PGOOptions> PGOOpt = std::nullopt,
139:       PassInstrumentationCallbacks *PIC = nullptr,
140:       IntrusiveRefCntPtr<vfs::FileSystem> FS = vfs::getRealFileSystem());
141: 
142:   /// Cross register the analysis managers through their proxies.
143:   ///
144:   /// This is an interface that can be used to cross register each
145:   /// AnalysisManager with all the others analysis managers.
146:   LLVM_ABI void
147:   crossRegisterProxies(LoopAnalysisManager &LAM, FunctionAnalysisManager &FAM,
148:                        CGSCCAnalysisManager &CGAM, ModuleAnalysisManager &MAM,
149:                        MachineFunctionAnalysisManager *MFAM = nullptr);
150: 
151:   /// Registers all available module analysis passes.
152:   ///
153:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \c
154:   /// ModuleAnalysisManager with all registered module analyses. Callers can
155:   /// still manually register any additional analyses. Callers can also
156:   /// pre-register analyses and this will not override those.
157:   LLVM_ABI void registerModuleAnalyses(ModuleAnalysisManager &MAM);
158: 
159:   /// Registers all available CGSCC analysis passes.
160:   ///
161:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \c CGSCCAnalysisManager
162:   /// with all registered CGSCC analyses. Callers can still manually register any
163:   /// additional analyses. Callers can also pre-register analyses and this will
164:   /// not override those.
165:   LLVM_ABI void registerCGSCCAnalyses(CGSCCAnalysisManager &CGAM);
166: 
167:   /// Registers all available function analysis passes.
168:   ///
169:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \c
170:   /// FunctionAnalysisManager with all registered function analyses. Callers can
171:   /// still manually register any additional analyses. Callers can also
172:   /// pre-register analyses and this will not override those.
173:   LLVM_ABI void registerFunctionAnalyses(FunctionAnalysisManager &FAM);
174: 
175:   /// Registers all available loop analysis passes.
176:   ///
177:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \c LoopAnalysisManager
178:   /// with all registered loop analyses. Callers can still manually register any
179:   /// additional analyses.
180:   LLVM_ABI void registerLoopAnalyses(LoopAnalysisManager &LAM);
181: 
182:   /// Registers all available machine function analysis passes.
183:   ///
184:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \c
185:   /// MachineFunctionAnalysisManager with all registered function analyses.
186:   /// Callers can still manually register any additional analyses. Callers can
187:   /// also pre-register analyses and this will not override those.
188:   LLVM_ABI void
189:   registerMachineFunctionAnalyses(MachineFunctionAnalysisManager &MFAM);
190: 
191:   /// Construct the core LLVM function canonicalization and simplification
192:   /// pipeline.
193:   ///
194:   /// This is a long pipeline and uses most of the per-function optimization
195:   /// passes in LLVM to canonicalize and simplify the IR. It is suitable to run
196:   /// repeatedly over the IR and is not expected to destroy important
197:   /// information about the semantics of the IR.
198:   ///
199:   /// Note that \p Level cannot be `O0` here. The pipelines produced are
200:   /// only intended for use when attempting to optimize code. If frontends
201:   /// require some transformations for semantic reasons, they should explicitly
202:   /// build them.
203:   ///
204:   /// \p Phase indicates the current ThinLTO phase.
205:   LLVM_ABI FunctionPassManager buildFunctionSimplificationPipeline(
206:       OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase);
207: 
208:   /// Construct the core LLVM module canonicalization and simplification
209:   /// pipeline.
210:   ///
211:   /// This pipeline focuses on canonicalizing and simplifying the entire module
212:   /// of IR. Much like the function simplification pipeline above, it is
213:   /// suitable to run repeatedly over the IR and is not expected to destroy
214:   /// important information. It does, however, perform inlining and other
215:   /// heuristic based simplifications that are not strictly reversible.
216:   ///
217:   /// Note that \p Level cannot be `O0` here. The pipelines produced are
218:   /// only intended for use when attempting to optimize code. If frontends
219:   /// require some transformations for semantic reasons, they should explicitly
220:   /// build them.
221:   ///
222:   /// \p Phase indicates the current ThinLTO phase.
223:   LLVM_ABI ModulePassManager buildModuleSimplificationPipeline(
224:       OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase);
225: 
226:   /// Construct the module pipeline that performs inlining as well as
227:   /// the inlining-driven cleanups.
228:   LLVM_ABI ModuleInlinerWrapperPass
229:   buildInlinerPipeline(OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase);
230: 
231:   /// Construct the module pipeline that performs inlining with
232:   /// module inliner pass.
233:   LLVM_ABI ModulePassManager
234:   buildModuleInlinerPipeline(OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase);
235: 
236:   /// Construct the core LLVM module optimization pipeline.
237:   ///
238:   /// This pipeline focuses on optimizing the execution speed of the IR. It
239:   /// uses cost modeling and thresholds to balance code growth against runtime
240:   /// improvements. It includes vectorization and other information destroying
241:   /// transformations. It also cannot generally be run repeatedly on a module
242:   /// without potentially seriously regressing either runtime performance of
243:   /// the code or serious code size growth.
244:   ///
245:   /// Note that \p Level cannot be `O0` here. The pipelines produced are
246:   /// only intended for use when attempting to optimize code. If frontends
247:   /// require some transformations for semantic reasons, they should explicitly
248:   /// build them.
249:   LLVM_ABI ModulePassManager buildModuleOptimizationPipeline(
250:       OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase LTOPhase);
251: 
252:   /// Build a per-module default optimization pipeline.
253:   ///
254:   /// This provides a good default optimization pipeline for per-module
255:   /// optimization and code generation without any link-time optimization. It
256:   /// typically correspond to frontend "-O[123]" options for optimization
257:   /// levels \c O1, \c O2 and \c O3 resp.
258:   LLVM_ABI ModulePassManager buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline(
259:       OptimizationLevel Level,
260:       ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase = ThinOrFullLTOPhase::None);
261: ", "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d@4152de6435984e9fc55431f4d74a7470d8ac05c0/-/blob/llvm/include/llvm/Passes/PassBuilder.h%3FL100-260"}, "isError": false}

{"meta": null, "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "{"content":"1: //===-------- LLVM-provided High-Level Optimization levels -*- C++ -*------===//\n2: //\n3: // Part of the LLVM Project, under the Apache License v2.0 with LLVM Exceptions.\n4: // See https://llvm.org/LICENSE.txt for license information.\n5: // SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception\n6: //\n7: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n8: /// \\file\n9: ///\n10: /// This header enumerates the LLVM-provided high-level optimization levels.\n11: /// Each level has a specific goal and rationale.\n12: ///\n13: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n14: \n15: #ifndef LLVM_PASSES_OPTIMIZATIONLEVEL_H\n16: #define LLVM_PASSES_OPTIMIZATIONLEVEL_H\n17: \n18: #include \"llvm/Support/Compiler.h\"\n19: #include \u003cassert.h\u003e\n20: \n21: namespace llvm {\n22: \n23: class OptimizationLevel final {\n24:   unsigned SpeedLevel = 2;\n25:   unsigned SizeLevel = 0;\n26:   OptimizationLevel(unsigned SpeedLevel, unsigned SizeLevel)\n27:       : SpeedLevel(SpeedLevel), SizeLevel(SizeLevel) {\n28:     // Check that only valid combinations are passed.\n29:     assert(SpeedLevel \u003c= 3 \u0026\u0026\n30:            \"Optimization level for speed should be 0, 1, 2, or 3\");\n31:     assert(SizeLevel \u003c= 2 \u0026\u0026\n32:            \"Optimization level for size should be 0, 1, or 2\");\n33:     assert((SizeLevel == 0 || SpeedLevel == 2) \u0026\u0026\n34:            \"Optimize for size should be encoded with speedup level == 2\");\n35:   }\n36: \n37: public:\n38:   OptimizationLevel() = default;\n39:   /// Disable as many optimizations as possible. This doesn't completely\n40:   /// disable the optimizer in all cases, for example always_inline functions\n41:   /// can be required to be inlined for correctness.\n42:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel O0;\n43: \n44:   /// Optimize quickly without destroying debuggability.\n45:   ///\n46:   /// This level is tuned to produce a result from the optimizer as quickly\n47:   /// as possible and to avoid destroying debuggability. This tends to result\n48:   /// in a very good development mode where the compiled code will be\n49:   /// immediately executed as part of testing. As a consequence, where\n50:   /// possible, we would like to produce efficient-to-execute code, but not\n51:   /// if it significantly slows down compilation or would prevent even basic\n52:   /// debugging of the resulting binary.\n53:   ///\n54:   /// As an example, complex loop transformations such as versioning,\n55:   /// vectorization, or fusion don't make sense here due to the degree to\n56:   /// which the executed code differs from the source code, and the compile\n57:   /// time cost.\n58:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel O1;\n59:   /// Optimize for fast execution as much as possible without triggering\n60:   /// significant incremental compile time or code size growth.\n61:   ///\n62:   /// The key idea is that optimizations at this level should \"pay for\n63:   /// themselves\". So if an optimization increases compile time by 5% or\n64:   /// increases code size by 5% for a particular benchmark, that benchmark\n65:   /// should also be one which sees a 5% runtime improvement. If the compile\n66:   /// time or code size penalties happen on average across a diverse range of\n67:   /// LLVM users' benchmarks, then the improvements should as well.\n68:   ///\n69:   /// And no matter what, the compile time needs to not grow superlinearly\n70:   /// with the size of input to LLVM so that users can control the runtime of\n71:   /// the optimizer in this mode.\n72:   ///\n73:   /// This is expected to be a good default optimization level for the vast\n74:   /// majority of users.\n75:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel O2;\n76:   /// Optimize for fast execution as much as possible.\n77:   ///\n78:   /// This mode is significantly more aggressive in trading off compile time\n79:   /// and code size to get execution time improvements. The core idea is that\n80:   /// this mode should include any optimization that helps execution time on\n81:   /// balance across a diverse collection of benchmarks, even if it increases\n82:   /// code size or compile time for some benchmarks without corresponding\n83:   /// improvements to execution time.\n84:   ///\n85:   /// Despite being willing to trade more compile time off to get improved\n86:   /// execution time, this mode still tries to avoid superlinear growth in\n87:   /// order to make even significantly slower compile times at least scale\n88:   /// reasonably. This does not preclude very substantial constant factor\n89:   /// costs though.\n90:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel O3;\n91:   /// Similar to \\c O2 but tries to optimize for small code size instead of\n92:   /// fast execution without triggering significant incremental execution\n93:   /// time slowdowns.\n94:   ///\n95:   /// The logic here is exactly the same as \\c O2, but with code size and\n96:   /// execution time metrics swapped.\n97:   ///\n98:   /// A consequence of the different core goal is that this should in general\n99:   /// produce substantially smaller executables that still run in\n100:   /// a reasonable amount of time.\n101:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel Os;\n102:   /// A very specialized mode that will optimize for code size at any and all\n103:   /// costs.\n104:   ///\n105:   /// This is useful primarily when there are absolute size limitations and\n106:   /// any effort taken to reduce the size is worth it regardless of the\n107:   /// execution time impact. You should expect this level to produce rather\n108:   /// slow, but very small, code.\n109:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel Oz;\n110: \n111:   bool isOptimizingForSpeed() const { return SizeLevel == 0 \u0026\u0026 SpeedLevel \u003e 0; }\n112: \n113:   bool isOptimizingForSize() const { return SizeLevel \u003e 0; }\n114: \n115:   bool operator==(const OptimizationLevel \u0026Other) const {\n116:     return SizeLevel == Other.SizeLevel \u0026\u0026 SpeedLevel == Other.SpeedLevel;\n117:   }\n118:   bool operator!=(const OptimizationLevel \u0026Other) const {\n119:     return SizeLevel != Other.SizeLevel || SpeedLevel != Other.SpeedLevel;\n120:   }\n121: \n122:   unsigned getSpeedupLevel() const { return SpeedLevel; }\n123: \n124:   unsigned getSizeLevel() const { return SizeLevel; }\n125: };\n126: } // namespace llvm\n127: \n128: #endif\n129: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d@4152de6435984e9fc55431f4d74a7470d8ac05c0/-/blob/llvm/include/llvm/Passes/OptimizationLevel.h"}", "annotations": null, "meta": null}], "structuredContent": {"content": "1: //===-------- LLVM-provided High-Level Optimization levels -*- C++ -*------===//
2: //
3: // Part of the LLVM Project, under the Apache License v2.0 with LLVM Exceptions.
4: // See https://llvm.org/LICENSE.txt for license information.
5: // SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception
6: //
7: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//
8: /// \file
9: ///
10: /// This header enumerates the LLVM-provided high-level optimization levels.
11: /// Each level has a specific goal and rationale.
12: ///
13: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//
14: 
15: #ifndef LLVM_PASSES_OPTIMIZATIONLEVEL_H
16: #define LLVM_PASSES_OPTIMIZATIONLEVEL_H
17: 
18: #include "llvm/Support/Compiler.h"
19: #include <assert.h>
20: 
21: namespace llvm {
22: 
23: class OptimizationLevel final {
24:   unsigned SpeedLevel = 2;
25:   unsigned SizeLevel = 0;
26:   OptimizationLevel(unsigned SpeedLevel, unsigned SizeLevel)
27:       : SpeedLevel(SpeedLevel), SizeLevel(SizeLevel) {
28:     // Check that only valid combinations are passed.
29:     assert(SpeedLevel <= 3 &&
30:            "Optimization level for speed should be 0, 1, 2, or 3");
31:     assert(SizeLevel <= 2 &&
32:            "Optimization level for size should be 0, 1, or 2");
33:     assert((SizeLevel == 0 || SpeedLevel == 2) &&
34:            "Optimize for size should be encoded with speedup level == 2");
35:   }
36: 
37: public:
38:   OptimizationLevel() = default;
39:   /// Disable as many optimizations as possible. This doesn't completely
40:   /// disable the optimizer in all cases, for example always_inline functions
41:   /// can be required to be inlined for correctness.
42:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel O0;
43: 
44:   /// Optimize quickly without destroying debuggability.
45:   ///
46:   /// This level is tuned to produce a result from the optimizer as quickly
47:   /// as possible and to avoid destroying debuggability. This tends to result
48:   /// in a very good development mode where the compiled code will be
49:   /// immediately executed as part of testing. As a consequence, where
50:   /// possible, we would like to produce efficient-to-execute code, but not
51:   /// if it significantly slows down compilation or would prevent even basic
52:   /// debugging of the resulting binary.
53:   ///
54:   /// As an example, complex loop transformations such as versioning,
55:   /// vectorization, or fusion don't make sense here due to the degree to
56:   /// which the executed code differs from the source code, and the compile
57:   /// time cost.
58:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel O1;
59:   /// Optimize for fast execution as much as possible without triggering
60:   /// significant incremental compile time or code size growth.
61:   ///
62:   /// The key idea is that optimizations at this level should "pay for
63:   /// themselves". So if an optimization increases compile time by 5% or
64:   /// increases code size by 5% for a particular benchmark, that benchmark
65:   /// should also be one which sees a 5% runtime improvement. If the compile
66:   /// time or code size penalties happen on average across a diverse range of
67:   /// LLVM users' benchmarks, then the improvements should as well.
68:   ///
69:   /// And no matter what, the compile time needs to not grow superlinearly
70:   /// with the size of input to LLVM so that users can control the runtime of
71:   /// the optimizer in this mode.
72:   ///
73:   /// This is expected to be a good default optimization level for the vast
74:   /// majority of users.
75:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel O2;
76:   /// Optimize for fast execution as much as possible.
77:   ///
78:   /// This mode is significantly more aggressive in trading off compile time
79:   /// and code size to get execution time improvements. The core idea is that
80:   /// this mode should include any optimization that helps execution time on
81:   /// balance across a diverse collection of benchmarks, even if it increases
82:   /// code size or compile time for some benchmarks without corresponding
83:   /// improvements to execution time.
84:   ///
85:   /// Despite being willing to trade more compile time off to get improved
86:   /// execution time, this mode still tries to avoid superlinear growth in
87:   /// order to make even significantly slower compile times at least scale
88:   /// reasonably. This does not preclude very substantial constant factor
89:   /// costs though.
90:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel O3;
91:   /// Similar to \c O2 but tries to optimize for small code size instead of
92:   /// fast execution without triggering significant incremental execution
93:   /// time slowdowns.
94:   ///
95:   /// The logic here is exactly the same as \c O2, but with code size and
96:   /// execution time metrics swapped.
97:   ///
98:   /// A consequence of the different core goal is that this should in general
99:   /// produce substantially smaller executables that still run in
100:   /// a reasonable amount of time.
101:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel Os;
102:   /// A very specialized mode that will optimize for code size at any and all
103:   /// costs.
104:   ///
105:   /// This is useful primarily when there are absolute size limitations and
106:   /// any effort taken to reduce the size is worth it regardless of the
107:   /// execution time impact. You should expect this level to produce rather
108:   /// slow, but very small, code.
109:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel Oz;
110: 
111:   bool isOptimizingForSpeed() const { return SizeLevel == 0 && SpeedLevel > 0; }
112: 
113:   bool isOptimizingForSize() const { return SizeLevel > 0; }
114: 
115:   bool operator==(const OptimizationLevel &Other) const {
116:     return SizeLevel == Other.SizeLevel && SpeedLevel == Other.SpeedLevel;
117:   }
118:   bool operator!=(const OptimizationLevel &Other) const {
119:     return SizeLevel != Other.SizeLevel || SpeedLevel != Other.SpeedLevel;
120:   }
121: 
122:   unsigned getSpeedupLevel() const { return SpeedLevel; }
123: 
124:   unsigned getSizeLevel() const { return SizeLevel; }
125: };
126: } // namespace llvm
127: 
128: #endif

These objects are set up by the RegisterPass\u003c\u003e\n27: /// template.\n28: ///\n29: class PassInfo {\n30: public:\n31:   using NormalCtor_t = Pass* (*)();\n32: \n33: private:\n34:   StringRef PassName;     // Nice name for Pass\n35:   StringRef PassArgument; // Command Line argument to run this pass\n36:   const void *PassID;\n37:   const bool IsCFGOnlyPass = false;      // Pass only looks at the CFG.\n38:   const bool IsAnalysis;                 // True if an analysis pass.\n39:   NormalCtor_t NormalCtor = nullptr;\n40: \n41: public:\n42:   /// PassInfo ctor - Do not call this directly, this should only be invoked\n43:   /// through RegisterPass.\n44:   PassInfo(StringRef name, StringRef arg, const void *pi, NormalCtor_t normal,\n45:            bool isCFGOnly, bool is_analysis)\n46:       : PassName(name), PassArgument(arg), PassID(pi), IsCFGOnlyPass(isCFGOnly),\n47:         IsAnalysis(is_analysis), NormalCtor(normal) {}\n48: \n49:   PassInfo(const PassInfo \u0026) = delete;\n50:   PassInfo \u0026operator=(const PassInfo \u0026) = delete;\n51: \n52:   /// getPassName - Return the friendly name for the pass, never returns null\n53:   StringRef getPassName() const { return PassName; }\n54: \n55:   /// getPassArgument - Return the command line option that may be passed to\n56:   /// 'opt' that will cause this pass to be run.  This will return null if there\n57:   /// is no argument.\n58:   StringRef getPassArgument() const { return PassArgument; }\n59: \n60:   /// getTypeInfo - Return the id object for the pass...\n61:   /// TODO : Rename\n62:   const void *getTypeInfo() const { return PassID; }\n63: \n64:   /// Return true if this PassID implements the specified ID pointer.\n65:   bool isPassID(const void *IDPtr) const { return PassID == IDPtr; }\n66: \n67:   bool isAnalysis() const { return IsAnalysis; }\n68: \n69:   /// isCFGOnlyPass - return true if this pass only looks at the CFG for the\n70:   /// function.\n71:   bool isCFGOnlyPass() const { return IsCFGOnlyPass; }\n72: \n73:   /// getNormalCtor - Return a pointer to a function, that when called, creates\n74:   /// an instance of the pass and returns it.  This pointer may be null if there\n75:   /// is no default constructor for the pass.\n76:   NormalCtor_t getNormalCtor() const {\n77:     return NormalCtor;\n78:   }\n79:   void setNormalCtor(NormalCtor_t Ctor) {\n80:     NormalCtor = Ctor;\n81:   }\n82: \n83:   /// createPass() - Use this method to create an instance of this pass.\n84:   Pass *createPass() const {\n85:     assert(NormalCtor \u0026\u0026\n86:            \"Cannot call createPass on PassInfo without default ctor!\");\n87:     return NormalCtor();\n88:   }\n89: };\n90: \n91: } // end namespace llvm\n92: \n93: #endif // LLVM_PASSINFO_H\n94: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d@4152de6435984e9fc55431f4d74a7470d8ac05c0/-/blob/llvm/include/llvm/PassInfo.h"}", "annotations": null, "meta": null}], "structuredContent": {"content": "1: //===- llvm/PassInfo.h - Pass Info class ------------------------*- C++ -*-===//
2: //
3: // Part of the LLVM Project, under the Apache License v2.0 with LLVM Exceptions.
4: // See https://llvm.org/LICENSE.txt for license information.
5: // SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception
6: //
7: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//
8: //
9: // This file defines and implements the PassInfo class.
10: //
11: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//
12: 
13: #ifndef LLVM_PASSINFO_H
14: #define LLVM_PASSINFO_H
15: 
16: #include "llvm/ADT/StringRef.h"
17: #include <cassert>
18: 
19: namespace llvm {
20: 
21: class Pass;
22: 
23: //===---------------------------------------------------------------------------
24: /// PassInfo class - An instance of this class exists for every pass known by
25: /// the system, and can be obtained from a live Pass by calling its
26: /// getPassInfo() method.  These objects are set up by the RegisterPass<>
27: /// template.
28: ///
29: class PassInfo {
30: public:
31:   using NormalCtor_t = Pass* (*)();
32: 
33: private:
34:   StringRef PassName;     // Nice name for Pass
35:   StringRef PassArgument; // Command Line argument to run this pass
36:   const void *PassID;
37:   const bool IsCFGOnlyPass = false;      // Pass only looks at the CFG.
38:   const bool IsAnalysis;                 // True if an analysis pass.
39:   NormalCtor_t NormalCtor = nullptr;
40: 
41: public:
42:   /// PassInfo ctor - Do not call this directly, this should only be invoked
43:   /// through RegisterPass.
44:   PassInfo(StringRef name, StringRef arg, const void *pi, NormalCtor_t normal,
45:            bool isCFGOnly, bool is_analysis)
46:       : PassName(name), PassArgument(arg), PassID(pi), IsCFGOnlyPass(isCFGOnly),
47:         IsAnalysis(is_analysis), NormalCtor(normal) {}
48: 
49:   PassInfo(const PassInfo &) = delete;
50:   PassInfo &operator=(const PassInfo &) = delete;
51: 
52:   /// getPassName - Return the friendly name for the pass, never returns null
53:   StringRef getPassName() const { return PassName; }
54: 
55:   /// getPassArgument - Return the command line option that may be passed to
56:   /// 'opt' that will cause this pass to be run.  This will return null if there
57:   /// is no argument.
58:   StringRef getPassArgument() const { return PassArgument; }
59: 
60:   /// getTypeInfo - Return the id object for the pass...
61:   /// TODO : Rename
62:   const void *getTypeInfo() const { return PassID; }
63: 
64:   /// Return true if this PassID implements the specified ID pointer.
65:   bool isPassID(const void *IDPtr) const { return PassID == IDPtr; }
66: 
67:   bool isAnalysis() const { return IsAnalysis; }
68: 
69:   /// isCFGOnlyPass - return true if this pass only looks at the CFG for the
70:   /// function.
71:   bool isCFGOnlyPass() const { return IsCFGOnlyPass; }
72: 
73:   /// getNormalCtor - Return a pointer to a function, that when called, creates
74:   /// an instance of the pass and returns it.  This pointer may be null if there
75:   /// is no default constructor for the pass.
76:   NormalCtor_t getNormalCtor() const {
77:     return NormalCtor;
78:   }
79:   void setNormalCtor(NormalCtor_t Ctor) {
80:     NormalCtor = Ctor;
81:   }
82: 
83:   /// createPass() - Use this method to create an instance of this pass.
84:   Pass *createPass() const {
85:     assert(NormalCtor &&
86:            "Cannot call createPass on PassInfo without default ctor!");
87:     return NormalCtor();
88:   }
89: };
90: 
91: } // end namespace llvm
92: 
93: #endif // LLVM_PASSINFO_H

Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the LLVM pass manager registration and execution chain. Let me synthesize all the findings:

## Key Files and Their Roles

### 1. PassManager Template Definition
- **`llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h`** - Core template definitions:
  - `PassManager<IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>` template class (line 162)
  - `AnalysisManager<IRUnitT, ExtraArgTs...>` template class (line 254)
  - `PassInfoMixin<DerivedT>` CRTP mixin (line 70)
  - `AnalysisInfoMixin<DerivedT>` CRTP mixin (line 93)
  - `addPass()` method on PassManager (line 195)
  - `registerPass()` method on AnalysisManager (line 472)
  - `ModulePassManager` typedef (line 238)
  - `FunctionPassManager` typedef (line 247)
  - `ModuleToFunctionPassAdaptor` class (line 850)

- **`llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerInternal.h`** - Internal implementation details:
  - `PassConcept<IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>` abstract base (line 40)
  - `PassModel<IRUnitT, PassT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>` concrete wrapper (line 72)
  - `AnalysisResultConcept<IRUnitT, InvalidatorT>` (line 121)
  - `AnalysisResultModel` (line 189)
  - `AnalysisPassConcept<IRUnitT, InvalidatorT, ExtraArgTs...>` (line 266)
  - `AnalysisPassModel<IRUnitT, PassT, InvalidatorT, ExtraArgTs...>` (line 287)

- **`llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerImpl.h`** - Template method implementations:
  - `PassManager<...>::run()` implementation (line 28)
  - `AnalysisManager<...>::getResultImpl()` (line 132)
  - `AnalysisManager<...>::invalidate()` (line 164)

- **`llvm/lib/IR/PassManager.cpp`** - Explicit template instantiations:
  - `PassManager<Module>`, `PassManager<Function>` (lines 22-23)
  - `AnalysisManager<Module>`, `AnalysisManager<Function>` (lines 24-25)

### 2. Legacy PassRegistry (for legacy pass manager)
- **`llvm/include/llvm/PassRegistry.h`** - `PassRegistry` class definition
- **`llvm/lib/IR/PassRegistry.cpp`** - `PassRegistry::registerPass()` implementation
- **`llvm/include/llvm/PassInfo.h`** - `PassInfo` class
- **`llvm/include/llvm/PassSupport.h`** - `RegisterPass<T>` template, `INITIALIZE_PASS_*` macros
- **`llvm/lib/IR/Pass.cpp`** - Pass infrastructure implementation

### 3. New Pass Manager Registration (PassBuilder)
- **`llvm/include/llvm/Passes/PassBuilder.h`** - `PassBuilder` class:
  - `registerModuleAnalyses()`, `registerFunctionAnalyses()`, etc.
  - `crossRegisterProxies()`
  - `buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline()`, `buildModuleSimplificationPipeline()`, `buildModuleOptimizationPipeline()`
  - `buildFunctionSimplificationPipeline()`

- **`llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilder.cpp`** - PassBuilder implementation:
  - `registerModuleAnalyses()` (line 649) - uses `PassRegistry.def` macros
  - `registerFunctionAnalyses()` (line 667)
  - `registerCGSCCAnalyses()` (line 658)
  - `registerLoopAnalyses()` (line 699)
  - `crossRegisterProxies()` (line 2568)

- **`llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp`** - Pipeline construction:
  - `buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline()` (line 1715) - main O2/O3 pipeline entry
  - `buildModuleSimplificationPipeline()` (line 1091)
  - `buildModuleOptimizationPipeline()` (line 1478)
  - `buildFunctionSimplificationPipeline()` (referenced)
  - `buildInlinerPipeline()` (line 942)
  - `addVectorPasses()` (line 1327)

- **`llvm/lib/Passes/PassRegistry.def`** - Pass registry definitions:
  - `MODULE_ANALYSIS`, `MODULE_PASS`, `FUNCTION_ANALYSIS`, `FUNCTION_PASS`, etc.

- **`llvm/include/llvm/Passes/OptimizationLevel.h`** - `OptimizationLevel` class (O0-O3, Os, Oz)

### 4. Specialized Pass Managers
- **`llvm/include/llvm/Transforms/Scalar/LoopPassManager.h`** - `LoopPassManager` specialization
- **`llvm/include/llvm/Analysis/CGSCCPassManager.h`** - CGSCC pass manager
- **`llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/MachinePassManager.h`** - Machine function pass manager
- **`llvm/lib/CodeGen/MachinePassManager.cpp`** - Machine pass manager implementation

### 5. Target-Specific Pass Registries
- **`llvm/include/llvm/Passes/TargetPassRegistry.inc`** - Target pass registry include
- **`llvm/include/llvm/Passes/MachinePassRegistry.def`** - Machine pass registry
- **`llvm/lib/Target/X86/X86PassRegistry.def`** - X86 passes
- **`llvm/lib/Target/AMDGPU/AMDGPUPassRegistry.def`** - AMDGPU passes
- **`llvm/lib/Target/NVPTX/NVPTXPassRegistry.def`** - NVPTX passes
- **`llvm/lib/Target/SPIRV/SPIRVPassRegistry.def`** - SPIRV passes
- **`llvm/lib/Target/DirectX/DirectXPassRegistry.def`** - DirectX passes

### 6. Extra Pass Manager Utilities
- **`llvm/include/llvm/Transforms/Utils/ExtraPassManager.h`** - `ExtraFunctionPassManager`, `ExtraLoopPassManager`

## The Registration and Execution Chain

### New Pass Manager (NPM) Chain:
1. **Pass Definition**: A pass class inherits from `PassInfoMixin<DerivedT>` and implements `run(IRUnitT&, AnalysisManagerT&)`.

2. **Registration in PassRegistry.def**: The pass is listed in `llvm/lib/Passes/PassRegistry.def` with a `MODULE_PASS`, `FUNCTION_PASS`, etc. macro.

3. **Analysis Registration**: `PassBuilder::registerModuleAnalyses()` (in `PassBuilder.cpp`) reads `PassRegistry.def` via `#include "PassRegistry.def"` with `MODULE_ANALYSIS` macro defined to call `MAM.registerPass([&] { return CREATE_PASS; })`.

4. **Pipeline Construction**: `PassBuilder::buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline(OptimizationLevel)` (in `PassBuilderPipelines.cpp`) calls:
   - `buildModuleSimplificationPipeline()` - early simplification
   - `buildModuleOptimizationPipeline()` - optimization (vectorization, etc.)
   - Each calls `MPM.addPass(SomePass())` to add passes to the pipeline

5. **Pass Addition**: `PassManager::addPass(PassT&&)` (in `PassManager.h`) wraps the pass in a `detail::PassModel<>` and pushes it to the `Passes` vector.

6. **Execution**: `PassManager::run(IRUnitT&, AnalysisManagerT&)` (in `PassManagerImpl.h`) iterates over `Passes` and calls `Pass->run(IR, AM, ExtraArgs...)` for each.

### Legacy Pass Manager Chain:
1. **Pass Definition**: A pass class inherits from `ModulePass`, `FunctionPass`, etc.
2. **Registration**: `INITIALIZE_PASS(passName, arg, name, cfg, analysis)` macro (in `PassSupport.h`) creates a `PassInfo` and calls `Registry.registerPass(*PI, true)`.
3. **Alternative**: `static RegisterPass<YourPass> X("name", "desc")` directly calls `PassRegistry::getPassRegistry()->registerPass(*this)`.
4. **Execution**: The legacy pass manager uses `PassRegistry` to look up and run passes.

  "text": "## LLVM Pass Manager Registration and Execution Chain

The LLVM codebase (sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d) contains two pass manager systems: the legacy pass manager and the new pass manager (NPM). The NPM is the primary system for O2/O3 optimization pipelines.

---

### 1. PassManager Template (Core Infrastructure)

**File: `llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h`**

The central template is `PassManager<IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>` (line 162), which inherits from `PassInfoMixin`. It stores passes as a `std::vector<std::unique_ptr<PassConceptT>>`. Key methods:
- `addPass(PassT&&)` (line 195): Wraps a pass in a `detail::PassModel<>` and appends it to the `Passes` vector.
- `run(IRUnitT&, AnalysisManagerT&, ExtraArgTs...)` (declared line 190, implemented in `PassManagerImpl.h`): Iterates over all passes and calls `Pass->run(IR, AM, ExtraArgs...)`.

Convenience typedefs:
- `ModulePassManager = PassManager<Module>` (line 238)
- `FunctionPassManager = PassManager<Function>` (line 247)

The `AnalysisManager<IRUnitT, ExtraArgTs...>` template (line 254) manages analysis results with lazy computation and caching. Its `registerPass(PassBuilderT&&)` method (line 472) accepts a callable (lambda) that constructs the analysis pass and stores it in a `DenseMap<AnalysisKey*, unique_ptr<PassConceptT>>`.

**File: `llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerInternal.h`**

Provides the concept-based polymorphism infrastructure:
- `PassConcept<IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>` (line 40): Abstract base with virtual `run()` and `name()` methods.
- `PassModel<IRUnitT, PassT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>` (line 72): Concrete wrapper that holds a pass instance and delegates `run()` to `Pass.run(IR, AM, ExtraArgs...)`.
- `AnalysisPassConcept` / `AnalysisPassModel`: Similar concept-based wrappers for analysis passes.
- `AnalysisResultConcept` / `AnalysisResultModel`: Wrappers for analysis results with `invalidate()` support.

**File: `llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerImpl.h`**

Contains the template method implementations:
- `PassManager::run()` (line 28): Iterates passes, calls `PI.runBeforePass()`, then `Pass->run(IR, AM, ExtraArgs...)`, then `AM.invalidate(IR, PassPA)`, then `PI.runAfterPass()`.
- `AnalysisManager::getResultImpl()` (line 132): Looks up or runs an analysis pass on demand.
- `AnalysisManager::invalidate()` (line 164): Invalidates stale analysis results after a pass runs.

**File: `llvm/lib/IR/PassManager.cpp`**

Contains explicit template instantiations for `PassManager<Module>`, `PassManager<Function>`, `AnalysisManager<Module>`, `AnalysisManager<Function>`, and proxy types.

---

### 2. Legacy PassRegistry (for the Legacy Pass Manager)

**File: `llvm/include/llvm/PassRegistry.h`**

Defines the `PassRegistry` class, a global singleton (accessed via `PassRegistry::getPassRegistry()`) that maps pass type IDs and argument strings to `PassInfo` objects.

**File: `llvm/lib/IR/PassRegistry.cpp`**

Implements `PassRegistry::registerPass(const PassInfo&, bool)` (line 48): Inserts the `PassInfo` into `PassInfoMap` (keyed by type ID) and `PassInfoStringMap` (keyed by argument string), then notifies listeners.

**File: `llvm/include/llvm/PassInfo.h`**

Defines `PassInfo`: stores pass name, argument string, type ID pointer, and a constructor function pointer (`NormalCtor_t`).

**File: `llvm/include/llvm/PassSupport.h`**

Provides two registration mechanisms:
1. `RegisterPass<passName>` template (line 89): Constructs a `PassInfo` and calls `PassRegistry::getPassRegistry()->registerPass(*this)` in its constructor. Used as a static global: `static RegisterPass<MyPass> X("mypass", "My Pass")`.
2. `INITIALIZE_PASS_BEGIN/END` macros (lines 39-54): Generate an `initialize<PassName>Pass(PassRegistry&)` function that creates a `PassInfo` and calls `Registry.registerPass(*PI, true)`. Used for passes with dependencies.

**File: `llvm/lib/IR/Pass.cpp`**

Implements `Pass::lookupPassInfo()` which queries `PassRegistry::getPassRegistry()->getPassInfo()`.

---

### 3. New Pass Manager Registration via PassBuilder

**File: `llvm/include/llvm/Passes/PassBuilder.h`**

Defines the `PassBuilder` class (line 114), which orchestrates pass registration and pipeline construction. Key methods:
- `registerModuleAnalyses(ModuleAnalysisManager&)` (line 157)
- `registerFunctionAnalyses(FunctionAnalysisManager&)` (line 173)
- `registerCGSCCAnalyses(CGSCCAnalysisManager&)` (line 165)
- `registerLoopAnalyses(LoopAnalysisManager&)` (line 180)
- `crossRegisterProxies(LAM, FAM, CGAM, MAM)` (line 147): Cross-registers analysis manager proxies.
- `buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline(OptimizationLevel, ThinOrFullLTOPhase)` (line 258): Builds the standard O1/O2/O3 pipeline.
- `buildModuleSimplificationPipeline(OptimizationLevel, ThinOrFullLTOPhase)` (line 223)
- `buildModuleOptimizationPipeline(OptimizationLevel, ThinOrFullLTOPhase)` (line 249)
- `buildFunctionSimplificationPipeline(OptimizationLevel, ThinOrFullLTOPhase)` (line 205)

**File: `llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilder.cpp`**

Implements the analysis registration functions. Each uses X-macro pattern with `PassRegistry.def`:
```cpp
void PassBuilder::registerModuleAnalyses(ModuleAnalysisManager &MAM) {
#define MODULE_ANALYSIS(NAME, CREATE_PASS) \
  MAM.registerPass([&] { return CREATE_PASS; });
#include "PassRegistry.def"
  for (auto &C : ModuleAnalysisRegistrationCallbacks) C(MAM);
}
```
Similarly for `registerFunctionAnalyses()`, `registerCGSCCAnalyses()`, `registerLoopAnalyses()`, and `registerMachineFunctionAnalyses()`.

`crossRegisterProxies()` (line 2568) registers inter-manager proxies (e.g., `FunctionAnalysisManagerModuleProxy`, `LoopAnalysisManagerFunctionProxy`) so that inner passes can access outer analysis managers.

**File: `llvm/lib/Passes/PassRegistry.def`**

The central X-macro registry file listing all built-in passes and analyses:
- `MODULE_ANALYSIS("name", CreatePass())` — module-level analyses
- `MODULE_PASS("name", CreatePass())` — module-level transformation passes
- `FUNCTION_ANALYSIS("name", CreatePass())` — function-level analyses
- `FUNCTION_PASS("name", CreatePass())` — function-level transformation passes
- `CGSCC_PASS`, `LOOP_PASS`, `LOOP_ANALYSIS`, etc.
- `MODULE_PASS_WITH_PARAMS("default", ..., buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline(L))` — the "default" pipeline alias

---

### 4. O2/O3 Pipeline Execution Order

**File: `llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp`**

**`buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline(OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase)`** (line 1715):

For O1/O2/O3 (non-O0), the pipeline is:
1. `MemProfRemoveInfo()` (non-LTO)
2. `Annotation2MetadataPass()` — convert annotations to metadata
3. `ForceFunctionAttrsPass()` — force function attributes
4. `invokePipelineStartEPCallbacks()` — extension point for plugins
5. `buildModuleSimplificationPipeline(Level, Phase)` — core simplification
6. `buildModuleOptimizationPipeline(Level, Phase)` — core optimization
7. `PseudoProbeUpdatePass()` (if PGO sample use)
8. `addAnnotationRemarksPass()` — emit annotation remarks
9. `addRequiredLTOPreLinkPasses()` (if LTO pre-link)

**`buildModuleSimplificationPipeline()`** (line 1091) includes:
- `InferFunctionAttrsPass()`, `CoroEarlyPass()`
- Early FPM: `LowerExpectIntrinsicPass`, `SimplifyCFGPass`, `SROAPass`, `EarlyCSEPass`, `CallSiteSplittingPass` (O3 only)
- `OpenMPOptPass`, `AttributorPass`, `IPSCCPPass`, `CalledValuePropagationPass`, `GlobalOptPass`
- PGO instrumentation/use passes
- `AlwaysInlinerPass`
- `buildInlinerPipeline()` or `buildModuleInlinerPipeline()` — main inliner
- `DeadArgumentEliminationPass`, `CoroCleanupPass`, `GlobalOptPass`, `GlobalDCEPass`

**`buildModuleOptimizationPipeline()`** (line 1478) includes:
- `PartialInlinerPass` (optional)
- `EliminateAvailableExternallyPass` (non-LTO)
- `ReversePostOrderFunctionAttrsPass`
- `RecomputeGlobalsAAPass`
- `invokeOptimizerEarlyEPCallbacks()` — extension point
- Function optimization pipeline (OptimizePM):
  - `Float2IntPass`, `LowerConstantIntrinsicsPass`
  - Loop passes: `LoopRotatePass`, `LoopDeletionPass`, `LoopInterchangePass`
  - `LoopDistributePass`, `InjectTLIMappings`
  - `addVectorPasses()`: `LoopVectorizePass`, `SLPVectorizerPass`, `VectorCombinePass`, `LoopUnrollPass`
  - `LoopSinkPass`, `InstSimplifyPass`, `DivRemPairsPass`, `TailCallElimPass`, `SimplifyCFGPass`
- `invokeOptimizerLastEPCallbacks()` — extension point
- `HotColdSplittingPass` (optional), `IROutlinerPass` (optional)
- `GlobalDCEPass`, `ConstantMergePass`, `MergeFunctionsPass` (optional)
- `CGProfilePass`, `RelLookupTableConverterPass`

---

### 5. Specialized Pass Managers

**`llvm/include/llvm/Transforms/Scalar/LoopPassManager.h`**: Defines `LoopPassManager` as a specialization of `PassManager<Loop, LoopAnalysisManager, LoopStandardAnalysisResults&, LPMUpdater&>`.

**`llvm/include/llvm/Analysis/CGSCCPassManager.h`**: Defines `CGSCCPassManager` for call-graph SCC passes.

**`llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/MachinePassManager.h`** and **`llvm/lib/CodeGen/MachinePassManager.cpp`**: Define `MachinePassManager` for machine-level passes.

**`llvm/include/llvm/Transforms/Utils/ExtraPassManager.h`**: Defines `ExtraFunctionPassManager<MarkerTy>` and `ExtraLoopPassManager<MarkerTy>` for conditional extra passes (e.g., post-vectorization cleanup).

---

### 6. Target-Specific Pass Registration

**`llvm/include/llvm/Passes/TargetPassRegistry.inc`**: A template include file used by targets to register their passes via `registerPassBuilderCallbacks()`. It calls `PB.registerAnalysisRegistrationCallback()` and `PB.registerPipelineParsingCallback()` using the target's `<Target>PassRegistry.def` file.

Target-specific `.def` files:
- `llvm/lib/Target/X86/X86PassRegistry.def`
- `llvm/lib/Target/AMDGPU/AMDGPUPassRegistry.def`
- `llvm/lib/Target/NVPTX/NVPTXPassRegistry.def`
- `llvm/lib/Target/SPIRV/SPIRVPassRegistry.def`
- `llvm/lib/Target/DirectX/DirectXPassRegistry.def`

**`llvm/include/llvm/Passes/MachinePassRegistry.def`**: Registry for machine-level passes.

---

### Summary of the Registration and Execution Chain

**New Pass Manager (NPM) — used for O2/O3:**
1. **Pass definition**: Inherit from `PassInfoMixin<DerivedT>` (in `PassManager.h`), implement `run(IRUnitT&, AnalysisManagerT&)`.
2. **Static registration**: List in `PassRegistry.def` with `MODULE_PASS("name", CreatePass())` or similar macro.
3. **Analysis registration**: `PassBuilder::registerModuleAnalyses()` (in `PassBuilder.cpp`) reads `PassRegistry.def` via X-macros and calls `MAM.registerPass([&]{ return CREATE_PASS; })` — which calls `AnalysisManager::registerPass()` (in `PassManager.h`) to store the pass in a `DenseMap`.
4. **Pipeline construction**: `PassBuilder::buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline(O2/O3)` (in `PassBuilderPipelines.cpp`) calls `MPM.addPass(SomePass())` — which calls `PassManager::addPass()` (in `PassManager.h`) to wrap the pass in a `PassModel<>` and push it to the `Passes` vector.
5. **Execution**: `PassManager::run(IR, AM)` (in `PassManagerImpl.h`) iterates the `Passes` vector and calls `Pass->run(IR, AM, ExtraArgs...)` for each pass in order.

**Legacy Pass Manager:**
1. **Pass definition**: Inherit from `ModulePass`, `FunctionPass`, etc. (in `Pass.h`).
2. **Registration**: Use `INITIALIZE_PASS(passName, arg, name, cfg, analysis)` macro (in `PassSupport.h`) which generates an `initializeXxxPass(PassRegistry&)` function that creates a `PassInfo` and calls `Registry.registerPass(*PI, true)` (in `PassRegistry.cpp`). Alternatively, use `static RegisterPass<MyPass> X("name", "desc")` which directly calls `PassRegistry::getPassRegistry()->registerPass(*this)`.
3. **Lookup**: `Pass::lookupPassInfo()` (in `Pass.cpp`) queries `PassRegistry::getPassRegistry()->getPassInfo()` to find registered passes by type ID or argument string."
}

  "text": "## LLVM Pass Manager Registration and Execution Chain

The LLVM codebase (sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d) contains two pass manager systems: the legacy pass manager and the new pass manager (NPM). The NPM is the primary system for O2/O3 optimization pipelines.

---

### 1. PassManager Template (Core Infrastructure)

**File: `llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h`**

The central template is `PassManager<IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>` (line 162), which inherits from `PassInfoMixin`. It stores passes as a `std::vector<std::unique_ptr<PassConceptT>>`. Key methods:
- `addPass(PassT&&)` (line 195): Wraps a pass in a `detail::PassModel<>` and appends it to the `Passes` vector.
- `run(IRUnitT&, AnalysisManagerT&, ExtraArgTs...)` (declared line 190, implemented in `PassManagerImpl.h`): Iterates over all passes and calls `Pass->run(IR, AM, ExtraArgs...)`.

Convenience typedefs:
- `ModulePassManager = PassManager<Module>` (line 238)
- `FunctionPassManager = PassManager<Function>` (line 247)

The `AnalysisManager<IRUnitT, ExtraArgTs...>` template (line 254) manages analysis results with lazy computation and caching. Its `registerPass(PassBuilderT&&)` method (line 472) accepts a callable (lambda) that constructs the analysis pass and stores it in a `DenseMap<AnalysisKey*, unique_ptr<PassConceptT>>`.

**File: `llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerInternal.h`**

Provides the concept-based polymorphism infrastructure:
- `PassConcept<IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>` (line 40): Abstract base with virtual `run()` and `name()` methods.
- `PassModel<IRUnitT, PassT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>` (line 72): Concrete wrapper that holds a pass instance and delegates `run()` to `Pass.run(IR, AM, ExtraArgs...)`.
- `AnalysisPassConcept` / `AnalysisPassModel`: Similar concept-based wrappers for analysis passes.
- `AnalysisResultConcept` / `AnalysisResultModel`: Wrappers for analysis results with `invalidate()` support.

**File: `llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerImpl.h`**

Contains the template method implementations:
- `PassManager::run()` (line 28): Iterates passes, calls `PI.runBeforePass()`, then `Pass->run(IR, AM, ExtraArgs...)`, then `AM.invalidate(IR, PassPA)`, then `PI.runAfterPass()`.
- `AnalysisManager::getResultImpl()` (line 132): Looks up or runs an analysis pass on demand.
- `AnalysisManager::invalidate()` (line 164): Invalidates stale analysis results after a pass runs.

**File: `llvm/lib/IR/PassManager.cpp`**

Contains explicit template instantiations for `PassManager<Module>`, `PassManager<Function>`, `AnalysisManager<Module>`, `AnalysisManager<Function>`, and proxy types.

---

### 2. Legacy PassRegistry (for the Legacy Pass Manager)

**File: `llvm/include/llvm/PassRegistry.h`**

Defines the `PassRegistry` class, a global singleton (accessed via `PassRegistry::getPassRegistry()`) that maps pass type IDs and argument strings to `PassInfo` objects.

**File: `llvm/lib/IR/PassRegistry.cpp`**

Implements `PassRegistry::registerPass(const PassInfo&, bool)` (line 48): Inserts the `PassInfo` into `PassInfoMap` (keyed by type ID) and `PassInfoStringMap` (keyed by argument string), then notifies listeners.

**File: `llvm/include/llvm/PassInfo.h`**

Defines `PassInfo`: stores pass name, argument string, type ID pointer, and a constructor function pointer (`NormalCtor_t`).

**File: `llvm/include/llvm/PassSupport.h`**

Provides two registration mechanisms:
1. `RegisterPass<passName>` template (line 89): Constructs a `PassInfo` and calls `PassRegistry::getPassRegistry()->registerPass(*this)` in its constructor. Used as a static global: `static RegisterPass<MyPass> X("mypass", "My Pass")`.
2. `INITIALIZE_PASS_BEGIN/END` macros (lines 39-54): Generate an `initialize<PassName>Pass(PassRegistry&)` function that creates a `PassInfo` and calls `Registry.registerPass(*PI, true)`. Used for passes with dependencies.

**File: `llvm/lib/IR/Pass.cpp`**

Implements `Pass::lookupPassInfo()` which queries `PassRegistry::getPassRegistry()->getPassInfo()`.

---

### 3. New Pass Manager Registration via PassBuilder

**File: `llvm/include/llvm/Passes/PassBuilder.h`**

Defines the `PassBuilder` class (line 114), which orchestrates pass registration and pipeline construction. Key methods:
- `registerModuleAnalyses(ModuleAnalysisManager&)` (line 157)
- `registerFunctionAnalyses(FunctionAnalysisManager&)` (line 173)
- `registerCGSCCAnalyses(CGSCCAnalysisManager&)` (line 165)
- `registerLoopAnalyses(LoopAnalysisManager&)` (line 180)
- `crossRegisterProxies(LAM, FAM, CGAM, MAM)` (line 147): Cross-registers analysis manager proxies.
- `buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline(OptimizationLevel, ThinOrFullLTOPhase)` (line 258): Builds the standard O1/O2/O3 pipeline.
- `buildModuleSimplificationPipeline(OptimizationLevel, ThinOrFullLTOPhase)` (line 223)
- `buildModuleOptimizationPipeline(OptimizationLevel, ThinOrFullLTOPhase)` (line 249)
- `buildFunctionSimplificationPipeline(OptimizationLevel, ThinOrFullLTOPhase)` (line 205)

**File: `llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilder.cpp`**

Implements the analysis registration functions. Each uses X-macro pattern with `PassRegistry.def`:
```cpp
void PassBuilder::registerModuleAnalyses(ModuleAnalysisManager &MAM) {
#define MODULE_ANALYSIS(NAME, CREATE_PASS) \
  MAM.registerPass([&] { return CREATE_PASS; });
#include "PassRegistry.def"
  for (auto &C : ModuleAnalysisRegistrationCallbacks) C(MAM);
}
```
Similarly for `registerFunctionAnalyses()`, `registerCGSCCAnalyses()`, `registerLoopAnalyses()`, and `registerMachineFunctionAnalyses()`.

`crossRegisterProxies()` (line 2568) registers inter-manager proxies (e.g., `FunctionAnalysisManagerModuleProxy`, `LoopAnalysisManagerFunctionProxy`) so that inner passes can access outer analysis managers.

**File: `llvm/lib/Passes/PassRegistry.def`**

The central X-macro registry file listing all built-in passes and analyses:
- `MODULE_ANALYSIS("name", CreatePass())` — module-level analyses
- `MODULE_PASS("name", CreatePass())` — module-level transformation passes
- `FUNCTION_ANALYSIS("name", CreatePass())` — function-level analyses
- `FUNCTION_PASS("name", CreatePass())` — function-level transformation passes
- `CGSCC_PASS`, `LOOP_PASS`, `LOOP_ANALYSIS`, etc.
- `MODULE_PASS_WITH_PARAMS("default", ..., buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline(L))` — the "default" pipeline alias

---

### 4. O2/O3 Pipeline Execution Order

**File: `llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp`**

**`buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline(OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase)`** (line 1715):

For O1/O2/O3 (non-O0), the pipeline is:
1. `MemProfRemoveInfo()` (non-LTO)
2. `Annotation2MetadataPass()` — convert annotations to metadata
3. `ForceFunctionAttrsPass()` — force function attributes
4. `invokePipelineStartEPCallbacks()` — extension point for plugins
5. `buildModuleSimplificationPipeline(Level, Phase)` — core simplification
6. `buildModuleOptimizationPipeline(Level, Phase)` — core optimization
7. `PseudoProbeUpdatePass()` (if PGO sample use)
8. `addAnnotationRemarksPass()` — emit annotation remarks
9. `addRequiredLTOPreLinkPasses()` (if LTO pre-link)

**`buildModuleSimplificationPipeline()`** (line 1091) includes:
- `InferFunctionAttrsPass()`, `CoroEarlyPass()`
- Early FPM: `LowerExpectIntrinsicPass`, `SimplifyCFGPass`, `SROAPass`, `EarlyCSEPass`, `CallSiteSplittingPass` (O3 only)
- `OpenMPOptPass`, `AttributorPass`, `IPSCCPPass`, `CalledValuePropagationPass`, `GlobalOptPass`
- PGO instrumentation/use passes
- `AlwaysInlinerPass`
- `buildInlinerPipeline()` or `buildModuleInlinerPipeline()` — main inliner
- `DeadArgumentEliminationPass`, `CoroCleanupPass`, `GlobalOptPass`, `GlobalDCEPass`

**`buildModuleOptimizationPipeline()`** (line 1478) includes:
- `PartialInlinerPass` (optional)
- `EliminateAvailableExternallyPass` (non-LTO)
- `ReversePostOrderFunctionAttrsPass`
- `RecomputeGlobalsAAPass`
- `invokeOptimizerEarlyEPCallbacks()` — extension point
- Function optimization pipeline (OptimizePM):
  - `Float2IntPass`, `LowerConstantIntrinsicsPass`
  - Loop passes: `LoopRotatePass`, `LoopDeletionPass`, `LoopInterchangePass`
  - `LoopDistributePass`, `InjectTLIMappings`
  - `addVectorPasses()`: `LoopVectorizePass`, `SLPVectorizerPass`, `VectorCombinePass`, `LoopUnrollPass`
  - `LoopSinkPass`, `InstSimplifyPass`, `DivRemPairsPass`, `TailCallElimPass`, `SimplifyCFGPass`
- `invokeOptimizerLastEPCallbacks()` — extension point
- `HotColdSplittingPass` (optional), `IROutlinerPass` (optional)
- `GlobalDCEPass`, `ConstantMergePass`, `MergeFunctionsPass` (optional)
- `CGProfilePass`, `RelLookupTableConverterPass`

---

### 5. Specialized Pass Managers

**`llvm/include/llvm/Transforms/Scalar/LoopPassManager.h`**: Defines `LoopPassManager` as a specialization of `PassManager<Loop, LoopAnalysisManager, LoopStandardAnalysisResults&, LPMUpdater&>`.

**`llvm/include/llvm/Analysis/CGSCCPassManager.h`**: Defines `CGSCCPassManager` for call-graph SCC passes.

**`llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/MachinePassManager.h`** and **`llvm/lib/CodeGen/MachinePassManager.cpp`**: Define `MachinePassManager` for machine-level passes.

**`llvm/include/llvm/Transforms/Utils/ExtraPassManager.h`**: Defines `ExtraFunctionPassManager<MarkerTy>` and `ExtraLoopPassManager<MarkerTy>` for conditional extra passes (e.g., post-vectorization cleanup).

---

### 6. Target-Specific Pass Registration

**`llvm/include/llvm/Passes/TargetPassRegistry.inc`**: A template include file used by targets to register their passes via `registerPassBuilderCallbacks()`. It calls `PB.registerAnalysisRegistrationCallback()` and `PB.registerPipelineParsingCallback()` using the target's `<Target>PassRegistry.def` file.

Target-specific `.def` files:
- `llvm/lib/Target/X86/X86PassRegistry.def`
- `llvm/lib/Target/AMDGPU/AMDGPUPassRegistry.def`
- `llvm/lib/Target/NVPTX/NVPTXPassRegistry.def`
- `llvm/lib/Target/SPIRV/SPIRVPassRegistry.def`
- `llvm/lib/Target/DirectX/DirectXPassRegistry.def`

**`llvm/include/llvm/Passes/MachinePassRegistry.def`**: Registry for machine-level passes.

---

### Summary of the Registration and Execution Chain

**New Pass Manager (NPM) — used for O2/O3:**
1. **Pass definition**: Inherit from `PassInfoMixin<DerivedT>` (in `PassManager.h`), implement `run(IRUnitT&, AnalysisManagerT&)`.
2. **Static registration**: List in `PassRegistry.def` with `MODULE_PASS("name", CreatePass())` or similar macro.
3. **Analysis registration**: `PassBuilder::registerModuleAnalyses()` (in `PassBuilder.cpp`) reads `PassRegistry.def` via X-macros and calls `MAM.registerPass([&]{ return CREATE_PASS; })` — which calls `AnalysisManager::registerPass()` (in `PassManager.h`) to store the pass in a `DenseMap`.
4. **Pipeline construction**: `PassBuilder::buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline(O2/O3)` (in `PassBuilderPipelines.cpp`) calls `MPM.addPass(SomePass())` — which calls `PassManager::addPass()` (in `PassManager.h`) to wrap the pass in a `PassModel<>` and push it to the `Passes` vector.
5. **Execution**: `PassManager::run(IR, AM)` (in `PassManagerImpl.h`) iterates the `Passes` vector and calls `Pass->run(IR, AM, ExtraArgs...)` for each pass in order.

**Legacy Pass Manager:**
1. **Pass definition**: Inherit from `ModulePass`, `FunctionPass`, etc. (in `Pass.h`).
2. **Registration**: Use `INITIALIZE_PASS(passName, arg, name, cfg, analysis)` macro (in `PassSupport.h`) which generates an `initializeXxxPass(PassRegistry&)` function that creates a `PassInfo` and calls `Registry.registerPass(*PI, true)` (in `PassRegistry.cpp`). Alternatively, use `static RegisterPass<MyPass> X("name", "desc")` which directly calls `PassRegistry::getPassRegistry()->registerPass(*this)`.
3. **Lookup**: `Pass::lookupPassInfo()` (in `Pass.cpp`) queries `PassRegistry::getPassRegistry()->getPassInfo()` to find registered passes by type ID or argument string."
}

arguments.message:
The task is complete. I have traced the LLVM pass manager registration and execution chain and created `/workspace/answer.json` with all findings.

## Summary of Findings

### Core PassManager Template Files
- **`llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h`** — `PassManager<IRUnitT>` template with `addPass()`, `AnalysisManager<IRUnitT>` with `registerPass()`, `PassInfoMixin`/`AnalysisInfoMixin` CRTP mixins
- **`llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerInternal.h`** — Concept-based polymorphism: `PassConcept`/`PassModel`, `AnalysisPassConcept`/`AnalysisPassModel`
- **`llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerImpl.h`** — `PassManager::run()` implementation (iterates passes, calls `Pass->run(IR, AM)`)
- **`llvm/lib/IR/PassManager.cpp`** — Explicit template instantiations

### Legacy PassRegistry
- **`llvm/include/llvm/PassRegistry.h`** + **`llvm/lib/IR/PassRegistry.cpp`** — `PassRegistry::registerPass()` global singleton
- **`llvm/include/llvm/PassInfo.h`** — `PassInfo` class
- **`llvm/include/llvm/PassSupport.h`** — `RegisterPass<T>` template + `INITIALIZE_PASS_*` macros

### New Pass Manager (O2/O3 Pipeline)
- **`llvm/include/llvm/Passes/PassBuilder.h`** — `PassBuilder` class with `registerModuleAnalyses()`, `buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline()`, etc.
- **`llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilder.cpp`** — Analysis registration via X-macros from `PassRegistry.def`; `crossRegisterProxies()`
- **`llvm/lib/Passes/PassRegistry.def`** — Central X-macro registry of all built-in passes/analyses
- **`llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp`** — `buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline()` → `buildModuleSimplificationPipeline()` + `buildModuleOptimizationPipeline()` with full O2/O3 pass ordering
- **`llvm/include/llvm/Passes/OptimizationLevel.h`** — `OptimizationLevel` (O0–O3, Os, Oz)

### Specialized Pass Managers
- `llvm/include/llvm/Transforms/Scalar/LoopPassManager.h` — `LoopPassManager`
- `llvm/include/llvm/Analysis/CGSCCPassManager.h` — CGSCC pass manager
- `llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/MachinePassManager.h` — Machine function pass manager
- Target-specific `.def` files for X86, AMDGPU, NVPTX, SPIRV, DirectX

## Summary of Findings

### Core PassManager Template Files
- **`llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h`** — `PassManager<IRUnitT>` template with `addPass()`, `AnalysisManager<IRUnitT>` with `registerPass()`, `PassInfoMixin`/`AnalysisInfoMixin` CRTP mixins
- **`llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerInternal.h`** — Concept-based polymorphism: `PassConcept`/`PassModel`, `AnalysisPassConcept`/`AnalysisPassModel`

### Legacy PassRegistry
- **`llvm/include/llvm/PassRegistry.h`** + **`llvm/lib/IR/PassRegistry.cpp`** — `PassRegistry::registerPass()` global singleton

### New Pass Manager (O2/O3 Pipeline)

- **`llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp`** — `buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline()` → `buildModuleSimplificationPipeline()` + `buildModuleOptimizationPipeline()` with full O2/O3 pass ordering

### Specialized Pass Managers
